methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Neura Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation. The peer universe comprises six same-stage private companies in robotics and manufacturing, ranging from $300M to $10.0B in valuation, alongside one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). The private peer set includes 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Peer-median analysis across the six comparable companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This method is applicable given the aligned sector and stage positioning. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the robotics sector at Series B maturity—1X Technologies commands a significant premium, while earlier-stage entrants cluster between $350M and $1.8B.

The triangulated valuation range supports $914M (low), $1.1B (median), and $1.2B (high). Neura Robotics' current valuation of €2.6B materially exceeds these peer-derived estimates, implying either a market-reflected premium or valuation compression risk. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, differentiated technology or market position not captured in stage-level comparables, and the appropriateness of the 1X Technologies outlier in the peer cohort. A single methodological input limits triangulation robustness.
